mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X4 M40i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X4 M40i engine typically costs £5,500–£8,500 supply only, with the B58 variant at the higher end. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500 depending on the specialist. In the UK, a full engine replacement for a BMW can range from £9,000 to £13,000. A reconditioned engine fully installed typically costs around £1,800–£2,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your X4.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X4 M40i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B58 is considered one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. It has gained a solid reputation for performance, smooth operation and tunability. The N55 (F26, 2015–2018) is also a proven, robust engine. Common issues across both engines include oil leaks (valve cover gasket, oil filter housing), VANOS solenoid failure, water pump failure, and HPFP issues. With proper maintenance, the B58 is a dependable performance engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X4 M40i?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common issues are oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing, VANOS solenoid failure (loss of power, rough idle), water pump and cooling system failures, high-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) issues, and turbocharger wastegate rattle. The B58 is generally reliable, with these issues being manageable with proper maintenance.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an X4 M40i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the generation and vehicle condition. Against a well-kept G02 worth £25,000–£45,000, a £7,000–£9,000 recon is 15–35% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired F26 worth £15,000–£20,000, the same recon may approach our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B58-engined cars command a premium due to improved reliability and performance.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X4 M40i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: F26 (2015–2018): N55B30T0. G02 (2018–present): B58B30M0 or B58B30O1. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation. If your car is registered after 2018, it's almost certainly the B58.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the B58 oil filter housing leak, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The oil filter housing gasket must be replaced — it's a known wear item on the B58. A replacement gasket costs £400–£900. If ignored, oil can leak onto the serpentine belt, causing it to slip or break. At the first sign of an oil leak around the oil filter housing, book an inspection immediately.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: X4 M40i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.problem: Oil leaks (valve cover, oil filter housing)

Viable repair — common on both engines

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X4 M40i? Check These First
buyIf: * G02 (2018+) with the B58 engine (modular design, improved reliability)
* Post-2019 B58TU1 with revised timing chain and fuel system
* Documented service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain and VANOS longevity)
* No oil leaks around the valve cover or oil filter housing
* Water pump replaced as preventative at 50k+ miles
avoidIf: * Oil leaks around the oil filter housing (belt damage risk on B58)
* Overheating history or unexplained coolant loss — cooling system may be compromised
* No service history — both N55 and B58 are sensitive to oil quality
* VANOS system failure symptoms — loss of power, rough idle

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X4 M40i is BMW's M Performance coupe-SUV — a 3.0-litre turbocharged straight-six powerhouse that delivers 360–387 hp and effortless torque. The F26 (2015–2018) uses the N55 — a proven single-turbo engine with a strong reliability record. The G02 (2018+) uses the B58 — part of BMW's modular engine family, widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. The B58 has gained a solid reputation for performance, smooth operation and tunability. Common issues across both engines include oil leaks (valve cover gasket, oil filter housing), VANOS solenoid failure, water pump failure, and HPFP issues. With typical X4 M40i values ranging from £20,000 (F26) to £45,000+ (G02), an engine replacement is a major financial commitment — but the B58 is generally reliable when properly maintained. A post-2019 B58TU1 with documented history is the safest choice.

cta.label: → Compare X4 M40i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X4 M40i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for smooth idle; any rattling or rough running may indicate VANOS or timing issues — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N55B30T0 or B58B30M0/O1) and oil-change discipline
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve cover and oil filter housing for leaks
* Coolant Level — Check reservoir when cold; any low or loss may indicate water pump or cooling system issues
* VANOS Health — Test acceleration; any loss of power or sluggish response may indicate VANOS solenoid wear
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or hesitation?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate HPFP, VANOS or sensor problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
